info.title: IntuneAppBuilder.Console
info.description: IntuneAppBuilder creates and deploys Intune packages for MSI and Win32 applications. This dotnet tool converts installation files into the .intunewin format that can then be published using the tool or uploaded manually into the Intune Portal.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParseResultExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ModelBindingCommandHandler.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: sources
options.description: Specifies a source to package. May be a directory with files for a Win32 app or a single msi file. May be specified multiple times.

options.name: output
options.description: Specifies an output directory for packaging artifacts. Each packaged application will exist as a raw intunewin file, a portal-ready portal.intunewin file, and an intunewin.json file containing metadata. Defaults to the working directory.

options.name: sources
options.description: Specifies a source to publish. May be a directory with *.intunewin.json files or a single json file

options.name: token
options.description: Specifies an access token to use when publishing.
